chatgpt.description: OpenAI’s flagship chat assistant: multi-turn conversations, optional browsing (plan/region-dependent), uploads, and light analysis. Great for drafting, code snippets, and study help—check the site for model names and policies.

claude.description: Anthropic’s assistant model known for long context and careful reasoning; strong for code review, technical writing, and long documents via web or API.

google-gemini.description: Google’s multimodal model family tied to Google accounts and Workspace; good for search-assisted answers, email drafts, and quick summaries where available.

microsoft-copilot.description: Microsoft’s assistant embedded across Windows, Edge, and Microsoft 365 for drafts, page summaries, and light automation—review enterprise data rules if you use M365.

deepseek.description: Chinese-friendly model stack with web chat and API; often praised for code/math/value pricing—confirm compliance for your region before production.

kimi.description: Moonshot’s Chinese-first assistant focused on long documents and link/file summaries—useful for papers, contracts, and long threads.

tongyi-qianwen.description: Alibaba’s model and app suite for chat, plugins, and industry scenarios—fits teams on Alibaba/DingTalk; individuals can try the web client.

ernie-bot.description: Baidu’s Chinese chat product with ties to search and Baidu services—check the site for current features and plans.

doubao.description: ByteDance’s assistant hub spanning chat and creative tools; handy for templates and domestic integrations—compare sub-products for export needs.

manus.description: General-purpose autonomous assistant product from Manus AI: delegates multi-step browser and research-style tasks behind a chat interface—check the site for task limits, regions, and data handling.

lovable.description: AI app builder from Lovable: prompt-first full-stack UI and backend scaffolding with iterative previews—verify export, hosting, and team permissions before committing to production workflows.

devin.description: Cognition’s “AI software engineer” product focused on multi-step coding tasks from tickets to pull requests—treat output as draft work; enforce human review, tests, security, and regional availability per your policy.

warp.description: Modern terminal with AI assistance for completions, shared commands, and workflow blocks—ideal for heavy CLI and SSH users; confirm what leaves your machine when using cloud-backed AI features.

zed.description: Rust-built code editor with low-latency editing and multiplayer collaboration plus optional AI hooks—compare extension depth vs VS Code before switching your whole team.

granola.description: Meeting notes product that pairs calendar context with transcription-friendly flows to turn calls into searchable summaries—validate retention, training use, and enterprise controls before rolling out company-wide.
